_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, averaging the prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice indexed to 2015 at 100, currently stands at 141, indicating a 41% increase since 2015. This rise reflects a substantial upward trend, but the climb has been uneven over time and varies by commodity. The index aggregates these diverse soft commodities to provide a broad sense of breakfast-related price changes globally. These figures stem from global commodity prices as tracked by FRED and the IMF.
notes.movers: The most significant contributors to the basket's increase are coffee and orange juice, which have risen by 99% and 34% respectively since 2015. Wheat and sugar showed more modest increases of 19% and 13%. These price movements are largely driven by specific weather events and disease outbreaks affecting production, such as frost and drought in coffee-producing arabica regions, and citrus greening and hurricanes impacting orange juice crops. Given that soft commodities often trade in relatively thin markets, these supply disruptions lead to pronounced price volatility rather than reflecting broad inflation trends.
notes.why: The headline rise in the basket is primarily driven by a few weather-sensitive crops vulnerable to climatic and biological shocks. Because of their exposure to sudden environmental changes, prices for these commodities can remain elevated over extended periods while markets adjust. This suggests that supply-side factors in thinly traded soft commodity markets continue to shape price trajectories more than general inflationary pressures.
